entities.label: IBM Research
entities.description: IBM Research is the research and development division for IBM, an American multinational information technology company. It is headquartered at the Thomas J. Watson Research Center in Yorktown Heights, New York, near IBM headquarters in Armonk, New York. IBM Research is the largest industrial research organization in the world, operating in over 170 countries with twelve laboratories on six continents.

entities.label: South America
entities.description: South America is a continent entirely in the Western Hemisphere and mostly in the Southern Hemisphere, with a considerably smaller portion in the Northern Hemisphere. It is bordered on the west by the Pacific Ocean, on the north and east by the Atlantic Ocean, and to the south by the Drake Passage.

entities.description: The quokka (Setonix brachyurus) is a small macropod about the size of a domestic cat. It is the only member of the genus Setonix. Like other marsupials in the macropod family (such as kangaroos and wallabies), the quokka is herbivorous and mainly nocturnal. Its range is a small area of southwestern Australia. They inhabit some smaller islands off the coast of Western Australia, particularly Rottnest Island just off Perth and Bald Island near Albany. Isolated, scattered populations also exist in forest and coastal heath between Perth and Albany. A small colony inhabits a protected area of Two Peoples Bay Nature Reserve, where they coexist with the critically endangered Gilbert's potoroo.

It is Australia's largest state by area and the second‑largest subdivision of any country on Earth.

entities.label: Rottnest Island
entities.description: Rottnest Island (Wajuk: Wadjemup), nicknamed "Rotto", is a 19‑square‑kilometre sandy, low‑lying island off the coast of Western Australia, 18 kilometres west of Fremantle. It is formed on a base of aeolianite limestone, is an A‑class reserve—the highest level of protection afforded to public land—and is a remnant of Pleistocene dune ridges. The island was first documented by Willem de Vlamingh in 1696 and was later used by British settlers for agriculture after the establishment of the Swan River Colony in 1829.

entities.label: heart attack
entities.description: A myocardial infarction (MI), commonly known as a heart attack, occurs when blood flow decreases or stops in one of the arteries of the heart, causing infarction (tissue death) to the heart muscle. The most common symptom is retrosternal chest pain or discomfort that classically radiates to the left shoulder, arm, or jaw. Other symptoms may include shortness of breath, nausea, feeling faint, a cold sweat, feeling tired, and decreased level of consciousness. An MI may cause heart failure, an irregular heartbeat, cardiogenic shock or cardiac arrest.
